source: On The Tape / RiskReversal — Fear is the market killer
summary: Wage inflation is the most dangerous and historically destructive component of inflation; outcome is highly uncertain
summary_extended: Baker argues that wage inflation is the most structurally dangerous component of the 2022 inflation episode, citing an unprecedented 1.4:1 ratio of job openings to job seekers as evidence of extreme labor market tightness. He warns that wage-price spirals have historically been among the most destructive economic forces across civilizations, making the current labor market dynamic uniquely threatening compared to other inflationary pressures. Baker explicitly acknowledges deep uncertainty about whether a full wage-price spiral will materialize, framing this as a moment requiring particular humility rather than confident prediction. The investment implication is implicitly cautious on risk assets broadly, but Baker stops short of identifying specific trades, making this a macro framework thesis rather than an actionable single-stock view. No specific equities, sectors, or instruments are named as beneficiaries or victims of this dynamic.
detail: Baker identifies the ratio of job openings to job seekers at an all-time high (1.4:1) as unprecedented. He warns that wage-price spirals have historically destroyed societies and civilizations, and that COVID has fundamentally reshaped the economic and social geography of America in ways not yet understood. He is explicitly humble about predicting whether a wage-price spiral will materialize.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as a 'moment for particular humility' and says it's 'really hard to predict a wage price spiral' while calling it the one thing that historically destroys societies.
quote_fragment: wage price spiral...historically over the long arc of history destroyed societies
